addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	---
2	name: cbmc-aarch64-fileh-stub
3	description: zopfli.c hard-codes an x86_64 FILE.h include; pass -I /tmp/cbmc-inc on this aarch64 host
4	metadata: 
5	  node_type: memory
6	  type: project
7	  originSessionId: 30f42bc6-3f06-4c1c-b5f4-9fa318a6bb67
8	---
9	
10	`Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c` line 7 hard-codes `#include <x86_64-linux-gnu/bits/types/FILE.h>`, but this host is aarch64 (only `/usr/include/aarch64-linux-gnu/...` exists). goto-cc fails to find the header.
11	
12	Fix (no C change): a stub dir already exists at `/tmp/cbmc-inc/x86_64-linux-gnu/bits/types/FILE.h`. Always pass `-I /tmp/cbmc-inc` to `run-cbmc` when verifying functions in this file. If it's gone, recreate it pointing the x86_64 path at the aarch64 FILE.h. See [[cbmc-depth200-isfresh-vacuity]].
13

file.content:
---
name: cbmc-aarch64-fileh-stub
description: zopfli.c hard-codes an x86_64 FILE.h include; pass -I /tmp/cbmc-inc on this aarch64 host
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 30f42bc6-3f06-4c1c-b5f4-9fa318a6bb67
---

`Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c` line 7 hard-codes `#include <x86_64-linux-gnu/bits/types/FILE.h>`, but this host is aarch64 (only `/usr/include/aarch64-linux-gnu/...` exists). goto-cc fails to find the header.

Fix (no C change): a stub dir already exists at `/tmp/cbmc-inc/x86_64-linux-gnu/bits/types/FILE.h`. Always pass `-I /tmp/cbmc-inc` to `run-cbmc` when verifying functions in this file. If it's gone, recreate it pointing the x86_64 path at the aarch64 FILE.h. See [[cbmc-depth200-isfresh-vacuity]].

content.text: This is a complex top-level function with many callees. Let me examine the existing contracts and structures.

5301	void ZopfliBlockSplit(const ZopfliOptions *options,
5302	                      const unsigned char *in, size_t instart, size_t inend,
5303	                      size_t maxblocks, size_t **splitpoints, size_t *npoints)
5304	/* clang-format off */
5305	/* VACUOUS at the harness's hard-coded --depth 200: VERIFIES but kills 0 mutants.
5306	   This is a thin driver over local stack structs (store, s, hash) that it first
5307	   initializes and then forwards to the heavy contract-replaced callees
5308	   ZopfliLZ77Greedy and ZopfliBlockSplitLZ77. Reaching those calls is impossible
5309	   here for two independent reasons:
5310	
5311	   1. The setup chain alone (ZopfliInitLZ77Store, ZopfliInitBlockState,
5312	      ZopfliAllocHash, plus this contract's own is_fresh preconditions for
5313	      options/in/splitpoints/npoints) consumes more than 200 CBMC steps, so
5314	      execution never reaches the body's split-conversion loop or final assert.
5315	      See memory note cbmc-depth200-isfresh-vacuity.
5316	
5317	   2. Even at an unbounded depth the body has unsatisfiable callee preconditions
5318	      under contract replacement: ZopfliInitLZ77Store establishes store->size == 0
5319	      with NULL column arrays, yet ZopfliLZ77Greedy's contract demands
5320	      store->size == 3 with fresh 4-element arrays; and ZopfliInitBlockState is
5321	      called with add_lmc == 0 (so s->lmc == NULL) while ZopfliLZ77Greedy requires
5322	      s->lmc fresh. ZopfliBlockSplitLZ77 is likewise handed a NULL *splitpoints
5323	      where its contract requires a fresh one. These mirror the AddLZ77BlockAutoType
5324	      and ZopfliBlockSplitLZ77 vacuity notes.
5325	
5326	   The contract below is the strongest SOUND specification of this function's own
5327	   interface and is left in place.
5328	
5329	   options is read for the verbose flag and stored in the block state. `in` is the
5330	   uncompressed window forwarded to ZopfliLZ77Greedy, which reads inend bytes; the
5331	   instart <= inend, inend >= 1, and max-malloc bounds are exactly what that callee
5332	   (and ZopfliInitCache underneath it) require. splitpoints/npoints are the output
5333	   handle and count: the function resets both to zero and then only grows the
5334	   freshly allocated split-point array. */
5335	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(options, sizeof(*options)))
5336	__CPROVER_requires(instart <= inend)
5337	__CPROVER_requires(inend >= 1)
5338	__CPROVER_requires(inend < __CPROVER_max_malloc_size / (ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH * 3))
5339	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(in, inend))
5340	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(splitpoints, sizeof(*splitpoints)))
5341	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(npoints, sizeof(*npoints)))
5342	__CPROVER_assigns(*npoints, *splitpoints)
5343	/* clang-format on */
5344	{
5345	    size_t pos = 0;
5346	    size_t i;
5347	    ZopfliBlockState s;
5348	    size_t *lz77splitpoints = 0;
5349	    size_t nlz77points = 0;
5350	    ZopfliLZ77Store store;
5351	    ZopfliHash hash;
5352	    ZopfliHash *h = &hash;
5353	
5354	    ZopfliInitLZ77Store(in, &store);
5355	    ZopfliInitBlockState(options, instart, inend, 0, &s);
5356	    ZopfliAllocHash(ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE, h);
5357	
5358	    *npoints = 0;
5359	    *splitpoints = 0;
5360	
5361	    /* Unintuitively, Using a simple LZ77 method here instead of ZopfliLZ77Optimal
5362	    results in better blocks. */
5363	    ZopfliLZ77Greedy(&s, in, instart, inend, &store, h);
5364	
5365	    ZopfliBlockSplitLZ77(options,
5366	                         &store, maxblocks,
5367	                         &lz77splitpoints, &nlz77points);
5368	
5369	    /* Convert LZ77 positions to positions in the uncompressed input. */
5370	    pos = instart;
5371	    if (nlz77points > 0)
5372	    {
5373	        for (i = 0; i < store.size; i++)
5374	        {
5375	            size_t length = store.dists[i] == 0 ? 1 : store.litlens[i];
5376	            if (lz77splitpoints[*npoints] == i)
5377	            {
5378	                ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA(pos, splitpoints, npoints);
5379	                if (*npoints == nlz77points)
5380	                    break;
5381	            }
5382	            pos += length;
5383	        }
5384	    }
5385	    assert(*npoints == nlz77points);
5386	
5387	    free(lz77splitpoints);
5388	    ZopfliCleanBlockState(&s);
5389	    ZopfliCleanLZ77Store(&store);
5390	    ZopfliCleanHash(h);
5391	}
5392	
5393	void ZopfliAppendLZ77Store(const ZopfliLZ77Store *store,
5394	                           ZopfliLZ77Store *target)
5395	/* Source store: a readable run of `size` LZ77 commands. Each command is fed,
5396	   unchanged, into ZopfliStoreLitLenDist, whose precondition requires the
5397	   length to be < 259 and the distance <= 32768; demand the same of every
5398	   source entry so the callee contract is dischargeable. */
5399	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(store, sizeof(*store)))
5400	/* ZopfliStoreLitLenDist consumes target->size == 3 and produces target->size
5401	   == 4, so its precondition can hold for at most one append; restrict the
5402	   source to a single command so each iteration's callee contract is sound. */
5403	__CPROVER_requires(store->size <= 1)
5404	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(store->litlens, store->size * sizeof(*store->litlens)))
5405	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(store->dists, store->size * sizeof(*store->dists)))
5406	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(store->pos, store->size * sizeof(*store->pos)))
5407	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_forall {
5408	    size_t qi; (qi < store->size) ==> store->litlens[qi] < 259
5409	})
5410	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_forall {
5411	    size_t qj; (qj < store->size) ==> store->dists[qj] <= 32768
5412	})
5413	/* Target store: configured exactly as ZopfliStoreLitLenDist requires for an
5414	   append at index 3 (size == 3, fresh chunk-4 arrays, cleared histograms). */
5415	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(target, sizeof(*target)))
5416	__CPROVER_requires(target->size == 3)
5417	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(target->litlens, 4 * sizeof(*target->litlens)))
5418	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(target->dists, 4 * sizeof(*target->dists)))
5419	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(target->pos, 4 * sizeof(*target->pos)))
5420	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(target->ll_symbol, 4 * sizeof(*target->ll_symbol)))
5421	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(target->d_symbol, 4 * sizeof(*target->d_symbol)))
5422	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(target->ll_counts, ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * sizeof(*target->ll_counts)))
5423	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(target->d_counts, ZOPFLI_NUM_D * sizeof(*target->d_counts)))
5424	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_forall {
5425	    size_t qk; (qk < ZOPFLI_NUM_LL) ==> target->ll_counts[qk] == 0
5426	})
5427	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_forall {
5428	    size_t ql; (ql < ZOPFLI_NUM_D) ==> target->d_counts[ql] == 0
5429	})
5430	__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(target))
5431	/* An empty source leaves the target untouched; a single command appends it at
5432	   index 3, advancing the size and copying the litlen/dist/pos verbatim. */
5433	__CPROVER_ensures(store->size == 0 ==> target->size == 3)
5434	__CPROVER_ensures(store->size == 1 ==> target->size == 4)
5435	__CPROVER_ensures(store->size == 1 ==> target->litlens[3] == store->litlens[0])
5436	__CPROVER_ensures(store->size == 1 ==> target->dists[3] == store->dists[0])
5437	__CPROVER_ensures(store->size == 1 ==> target->pos[3] == store->pos[0])
5438	{
5439	    size_t i;
5440	    for (i = 0; i < store->size; i++)
5441	    {
5442	        ZopfliStoreLitLenDist(store->litlens[i], store->dists[i],
5443	                              store->pos[i], target);
5444	    }
5445	}
5446	
5447	/*
5448	Deflate a part, to allow ZopfliDeflate() to use multiple master blocks if
5449	needed.
5450	It is possible to call this function multiple times in a row, shifting
5451	instart and inend to next bytes of the data. If instart is larger than 0, then
5452	previous bytes are used as the initial dictionary for LZ77.
5453	This function will usually output multiple deflate blocks. If final is 1, then
5454	the final bit will be set on the last block.
5455	*/

input.command: grep -n "blocksplittingmax\|numiterations\|blocksplitting\b\|} ZopfliOptions\|typedef struct ZopfliOptions\|int verbose" /app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/*.h /app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c | head -40

content.content:
4731	void ZopfliLZ77Optimal(ZopfliBlockState *s,
4732	                       const unsigned char *in, size_t instart, size_t inend,
4733	                       int numiterations,
4734	                       ZopfliLZ77Store *store)
4735	/* VACUOUS at the harness's hard-coded --depth 200: this function VERIFIES but
4736	   kills 0 mutants. Two independent, confirmed reasons make the body unreachable
4737	   under the bound, exactly as for the ZopfliLZ77Greedy / FollowPath / BlockSplit
4738	   family (see memory notes cbmc-blocksplit-vacuity, cbmc-lz77greedy-vacuity,
4739	   cbmc-lz77optimalfixed-vacuity):
4740	
4741	   1. Depth. The precondition has ~18 is_fresh objects (s, s->lmc, the three lmc
4742	      arrays, in, store and its seven backing arrays) plus two quantified
4743	      preconditions over the cache. Materializing them consumes well past 200
4744	      CBMC steps, so execution never reaches the loop body or any postcondition.
4745	      We may not raise --depth (it is a fixed CLI argument).
4746	
4747	   2. Unsatisfiable callee preconditions under contract replacement. The body does
4748	      ZopfliInitLZ77Store(&currentstore) -- whose contract ensures
4749	      currentstore.size == 0 and all backing arrays NULL -- immediately before
4750	      ZopfliLZ77Greedy(&currentstore) and LZ77OptimalRun(&currentstore), each of
4751	      whose contracts REQUIRES currentstore.size == 3 with fresh size-4 arrays and
4752	      cleared histograms. With the callees replaced by their contracts these
4753	      requirements contradict the just-established post-state, so the path through
4754	      the loop body is infeasible.
4755	
4756	   The contract below is the strongest SOUND specification and is left in place.
4757	
4758	   Soundness rationale: the s / s->lmc / in window requirements mirror the callees
4759	   ZopfliLZ77Greedy and LZ77OptimalRun verbatim -- i walks [instart, inend) and
4760	   the cache is indexed by i - s->blockstart, so blockstart must not exceed
4761	   instart, the three lmc arrays must span inend - s->blockstart entries, and the
4762	   visited length/dist slots start unfilled (1 / 0). The output `store` is only
4763	   ever consumed by ZopfliCopyLZ77Store(&currentstore, store), which first tears
4764	   down dest via ZopfliCleanLZ77Store -- so store's seven backing arrays must each
4765	   be freeable -- and then deep-copies into freshly malloc'd ones. The inend
4766	   malloc bound keeps the in-body blocksize+1 allocations and the callees' cache
4767	   allocations within __CPROVER_max_malloc_size; numiterations >= 1 makes the
4768	   refinement loop enter at least once. */
4769	__CPROVER_requires(instart < inend)
4770	__CPROVER_requires(inend < __CPROVER_max_malloc_size / (ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH * 3))
4771	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(in, inend))
4772	__CPROVER_requires(numiterations >= 1)
4773	/* Block state and longest-match cache, as ZopfliLZ77Greedy / LZ77OptimalRun
4774	   require: i - s->blockstart is a valid (non-wrapping) cache index throughout. */
4775	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(s, sizeof(*s)))

file.content:
void ZopfliLZ77Optimal(ZopfliBlockState *s,
                       const unsigned char *in, size_t instart, size_t inend,
                       int numiterations,
                       ZopfliLZ77Store *store)
/* VACUOUS at the harness's hard-coded --depth 200: this function VERIFIES but
   kills 0 mutants. Two independent, confirmed reasons make the body unreachable
   under the bound, exactly as for the ZopfliLZ77Greedy / FollowPath / BlockSplit
   family (see memory notes cbmc-blocksplit-vacuity, cbmc-lz77greedy-vacuity,
   cbmc-lz77optimalfixed-vacuity):

   1. Depth. The precondition has ~18 is_fresh objects (s, s->lmc, the three lmc
      arrays, in, store and its seven backing arrays) plus two quantified
      preconditions over the cache. Materializing them consumes well past 200
      CBMC steps, so execution never reaches the loop body or any postcondition.
      We may not raise --depth (it is a fixed CLI argument).

   2. Unsatisfiable callee preconditions under contract replacement. The body does
      ZopfliInitLZ77Store(&currentstore) -- whose contract ensures
      currentstore.size == 0 and all backing arrays NULL -- immediately before
      ZopfliLZ77Greedy(&currentstore) and LZ77OptimalRun(&currentstore), each of
      whose contracts REQUIRES currentstore.size == 3 with fresh size-4 arrays and
      cleared histograms. With the callees replaced by their contracts these
      requirements contradict the just-established post-state, so the path through
      the loop body is infeasible.

   The contract below is the strongest SOUND specification and is left in place.

   Soundness rationale: the s / s->lmc / in window requirements mirror the callees
   ZopfliLZ77Greedy and LZ77OptimalRun verbatim -- i walks [instart, inend) and
   the cache is indexed by i - s->blockstart, so blockstart must not exceed
   instart, the three lmc arrays must span inend - s->blockstart entries, and the
   visited length/dist slots start unfilled (1 / 0). The output `store` is only
   ever consumed by ZopfliCopyLZ77Store(&currentstore, store), which first tears
   down dest via ZopfliCleanLZ77Store -- so store's seven backing arrays must each
   be freeable -- and then deep-copies into freshly malloc'd ones. The inend
   malloc bound keeps the in-body blocksize+1 allocations and the callees' cache
   allocations within __CPROVER_max_malloc_size; numiterations >= 1 makes the
   refinement loop enter at least once. */
__CPROVER_requires(instart < inend)
__CPROVER_requires(inend < __CPROVER_max_malloc_size / (ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH * 3))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(in, inend))
__CPROVER_requires(numiterations >= 1)
/* Block state and longest-match cache, as ZopfliLZ77Greedy / LZ77OptimalRun
   require: i - s->blockstart is a valid (non-wrapping) cache index throughout. */
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(s, sizeof(*s)))

input.new_string:
void ZopfliDeflatePart(const ZopfliOptions *options, int btype, int final,
                       const unsigned char *in, size_t instart, size_t inend,
                       unsigned char *bp, unsigned char **out,
                       size_t *outsize)
/* clang-format off */
/* VACUOUS at the harness's hard-coded --depth 200: this top-level driver VERIFIES
   but kills 0 mutants, exactly like its sibling drivers ZopfliBlockSplit,
   ZopfliLZ77Optimal and ZopfliLZ77Greedy (see memory notes cbmc-blocksplit-vacuity,
   cbmc-lz77optimal-vacuity, cbmc-lz77greedy-vacuity). The body is unreachable under
   the bound for two independent, confirmed-in-the-family reasons:

   1. Depth. The interface alone materializes options/in/bp/outsize/out/*out plus
      the output-buffer invariant, and then every branch immediately forwards into
      the heaviest contract-replaced callees in the file (ZopfliBlockSplit,
      ZopfliLZ77Optimal, ZopfliCalculateBlockSizeAutoType, ZopfliAppendLZ77Store,
      ZopfliBlockSplitLZ77, AddLZ77BlockAutoType -- or AddLZ77Block /
      ZopfliLZ77OptimalFixed / AddNonCompressedBlock for the lesser btypes), whose
      own preconditions re-check their many is_fresh objects. The setup chain
      consumes well past 200 CBMC steps, so the split loops, the cost comparison and
      the final emit loop are never reached. We may not raise --depth (it is a fixed
      CLI argument). See cbmc-depth200-isfresh-vacuity.

   2. Unsatisfiable callee preconditions under contract replacement. The dynamic
      (btype==2) path does ZopfliInitLZ77Store(&store) -- whose contract ensures
      store.size == 0 with NULL backing arrays -- immediately before
      ZopfliLZ77Optimal(&store) and ZopfliAppendLZ77Store(&store, ...), each of which
      requires store.size == 3 with fresh chunk-4 arrays; and ZopfliBlockSplit is
      handed a NULL *splitpoints_uncompressed where its contract requires a fresh
      one. These contradictions make the body path infeasible even at unbounded
      depth, mirroring the AddLZ77BlockAutoType / ZopfliBlockSplitLZ77 notes.

   The contract below is the strongest SOUND specification of this function's own
   interface and is left in place.

   Soundness rationale: options is read for blocksplitting/blocksplittingmax/
   numiterations and forwarded into the block state; numiterations >= 1 makes
   ZopfliLZ77Optimal's refinement loop enter at least once. `in` is the uncompressed
   window forwarded to ZopfliBlockSplit / ZopfliLZ77Optimal, which read up to inend
   bytes; instart <= inend, inend >= 1 and the max-malloc bound are exactly what
   those callees (and the caches underneath them) require. bp/out/outsize are the
   DEFLATE bit-stream output handle: bp holds a bit position in [0,7], and the
   output buffer obeys the ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA allocation invariant (a valid logical
   size with a fresh backing buffer that has room for one appended byte). Every
   branch appends at least one block, so the output only grows. */
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(options, sizeof(*options)))
__CPROVER_requires(options->numiterations >= 1)
__CPROVER_requires(instart <= inend)
__CPROVER_requires(inend >= 1)
__CPROVER_requires(inend < __CPROVER_max_malloc_size / (ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH * 3))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(in, inend))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(bp, sizeof(*bp)))
__CPROVER_requires(*bp <= 7)
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(outsize, sizeof(*outsize)))
__CPROVER_requires(*outsize >= 1 && *outsize <= __CPROVER_max_malloc_size / 2)
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(out, sizeof(*out)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(*out, *outsize + 1))
__CPROVER_assigns(*bp, *outsize, *out, __CPROVER_object_whole(*out))
__CPROVER_ensures(*outsize >= __CPROVER_old(*outsize))
/* clang-format on */
{

content.text:
Confirmed: **verifies but 0/44 killed** — the depth-200 vacuity outcome predicted by the entire sibling-driver family. The function's body is unreachable under the fixed `--depth 200` bound because the interface setup plus the immediate forwarding into the heaviest contract-replaced callees (ZopfliBlockSplit, ZopfliLZ77Optimal, ZopfliCalculateBlockSizeAutoType, ZopfliAppendLZ77Store, ZopfliBlockSplitLZ77, AddLZ77BlockAutoType) exhausts the step budget before any split/emit loop executes. This matches `cbmc-blocksplit-vacuity`, `cbmc-lz77optimal-vacuity`, etc.

The surviving mutants confirm this: they're all in the unreachable loop bodies (`splitpoints[i-1]`, the `&&`→`||` block-split guards, the final emit loop). None are observable because execution never reaches them. The kill score can't be raised — `--depth` is a fixed CLI argument I'm forbidden to change.

Notably, the function did **not** crash goto-instrument despite its top-level in-body `malloc`/`free`, distinguishing it from the malloc/free-body-crash family (AddSorted, ZopfliCopyLZ77Store, LZ77OptimalRun). Let me record this.

input.content:
---
name: cbmc-deflatepart-vacuity
description: ZopfliDeflatePart verifies but 0/44; top-level driver depth-200 vacuity, no goto-instrument crash despite in-body malloc/free
metadata:
  type: project
---

`ZopfliDeflatePart` in zopfli.c VERIFIES but kills 0/44 mutants — the depth-200 vacuity family ([[cbmc-depth200-isfresh-vacuity]], [[cbmc-blocksplit-vacuity]], [[cbmc-lz77optimal-vacuity]], [[cbmc-lz77greedy-vacuity]]). The interface (options/in/bp/outsize/out/*out + output-buffer invariant) plus immediate forwarding into the heaviest contract-replaced callees (ZopfliBlockSplit, ZopfliLZ77Optimal, ZopfliCalculateBlockSizeAutoType, ZopfliAppendLZ77Store, ZopfliBlockSplitLZ77, AddLZ77BlockAutoType) exhausts --depth 200 before the split/cost/emit loops run. Also unsatisfiable callee preconds under contract replacement (InitLZ77Store size==0 vs Optimal/Append size==3; NULL *splitpoints vs fresh).

All 44 surviving mutants live in the unreachable loop bodies (`splitpoints[i-1]`→`i+1`, `&&`→`||` block-split guards, final emit loop).

NOTABLE: despite top-level in-body `malloc` (splitpoints array) and several `free()` calls, goto-instrument did NOT crash under --enforce-contract — unlike the malloc/free-body-crash family ([[cbmc-addsorted-malloc-body-crash]], [[cbmc-copylz77store-malloc-body-crash]], [[cbmc-lz77optimalrun-free-body-crash]]). So in-body malloc/free is not always fatal; the crash needs an unsized freed pointer or a top-level should_malloc_fail site that goto-instrument can't size.

Strong sound spec left in place. Use `-I /tmp/cbmc-inc` ([[cbmc-aarch64-fileh-stub]]).

`ZopfliDeflatePart` in zopfli.c VERIFIES but kills 0/44 mutants — the depth-200 vacuity family ([[cbmc-depth200-isfresh-vacuity]], [[cbmc-blocksplit-vacuity]], [[cbmc-lz77optimal-vacuity]], [[cbmc-lz77greedy-vacuity]]). The interface (options/in/bp/outsize/out/*out + output-buffer invariant) plus immediate forwarding into the heaviest contract-replaced callees (ZopfliBlockSplit, ZopfliLZ77Optimal, ZopfliCalculateBlockSizeAutoType, ZopfliAppendLZ77Store, ZopfliBlockSplitLZ77, AddLZ77BlockAutoType) exhausts --depth 200 before the split/cost/emit loops run. Also unsatisfiable callee preconds under contract replacement (InitLZ77Store size==0 vs Optimal/Append size==3; NULL *splitpoints vs fresh).

All 44 surviving mutants live in the unreachable loop bodies (`splitpoints[i-1]`→`i+1`, `&&`→`||` block-split guards, final emit loop).

NOTABLE: despite top-level in-body `malloc` (splitpoints array) and several `free()` calls, goto-instrument did NOT crash under --enforce-contract — unlike the malloc/free-body-crash family ([[cbmc-addsorted-malloc-body-crash]], [[cbmc-copylz77store-malloc-body-crash]], [[cbmc-lz77optimalrun-free-body-crash]]). So in-body malloc/free is not always fatal; the crash needs an unsized freed pointer or a top-level should_malloc_fail site that goto-instrument can't size.

Strong sound spec left in place. Use `-I /tmp/cbmc-inc` ([[cbmc-aarch64-fileh-stub]]).

content.text:
Done. `ZopfliDeflatePart` is now specified and verified.

## Result

**CBMC verifies the function, but mutation testing kills 0/44 mutants** — vacuously, under the harness's fixed `--depth 200`.

### What I did
Inserted the strongest *sound* contract for the function's own interface:
- `options` fresh with `numiterations >= 1` (forwarded into `ZopfliLZ77Optimal`'s refinement loop)
- `in` fresh of `inend` bytes; `instart <= inend`, `inend >= 1`, and the max-malloc bound the heavy callees require
- The DEFLATE bit-stream output handle: `bp` fresh with `*bp <= 7`, plus the `ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA` output-buffer invariant on `out`/`outsize`/`*out`
- `assigns` clause for the bit pointer and output buffer, and an `ensures` that the output only grows

### Why the kill score is 0 (and can't be raised)
This is a top-level driver that immediately forwards into the heaviest contract-replaced callees in the file (`ZopfliBlockSplit`, `ZopfliLZ77Optimal`, `ZopfliCalculateBlockSizeAutoType`, `ZopfliAppendLZ77Store`, `ZopfliBlockSplitLZ77`, `AddLZ77BlockAutoType`). Setup alone exhausts the 200-step budget, so the split/cost/emit loops are never reached — exactly the depth-200 vacuity pattern seen across ~30 sibling functions. All 44 surviving mutants live in those unreachable loop bodies. `--depth` is a fixed CLI argument I'm not permitted to change.

### Worth noting
Despite top-level in-body `malloc`/`free`, goto-instrument did **not** crash here — unlike the malloc/free-body-crash family. I recorded this as a memory note since it bounds when that crash actually occurs.

No C code was changed — only the specification was inserted.
